author: David Reed
summary: One change. chg-7: per-run IntentRecord (governance rollout P-3 / T-16). A typed, record-only intent contract emitted as the FIRST audit event (intent.declared) of every prior-auth run, so P-4 (scope guard) and P-5 (evidence-grounding detector) can read and cite it. Observability only -- no enforcement, no agent-decision surface touched.

changes.description: Add IntentRecord (src/pacca/models/intent.py); emit it as action=intent.declared, the FIRST audit event of every run, from the submission route before authorization_submitted. Record-only: writes correlation_id, request_id, subject_ref(=patient_id), purpose, and declared-constant allowed_collections/actions/expected_effects/limits into the existing AuditLogModel.details JSON -- no migration. In the route (not orchestrator) since it owns event #0, so every trail begins with intent.declared.
changes.failure_pattern: There is no first-class, typed record of what a run is permitted to touch or expected to do. P-4 (minimum-necessary scope guard) and P-5 (evidence-grounding detector) need a per-run intent to read, enforce against, and cite; without it intent is implicit -- scattered across request fields and orchestrator constants -- and unauditable at run start.
changes.root_cause: Intent was never modeled. The audit trail began with authorization_submitted (a receipt of what arrived), not a declaration of the run's scope and expected effect. CausalGate's typed intent contract supplies the pattern; PACCA needs the scope + expected-effects fields (the per-request purpose is fixed at prior-auth adjudication).

evidence.note: New test_first_audit_record_is_intent_declared asserts every run begins with intent.declared and that details carry purpose / request_id / subject_ref / allowed_collections / allowed_actions, sharing the run correlation_id. Existing test_submission_audit_has_correct_action updated: intent.declared is event [0], authorization_submitted is [1]. 6/6 audit-trail tests pass; full deterministic suite 651 passed, 3 deselected; ruff + mypy clean.

IntentRecord Pydantic v2 model; allowed_* / expected_effects / limits declared as module-level constants (SSOT) since no runtime capability/RBAC source exists yet; for_prior_auth(correlation_id, request_id, subject_ref) factory keeps the route call-site thin.

changes.why_this_component: The change's only surface is the audit trail's first event -- an observability / audit-schema concern, not an agent decision surface. No prompt, tool, escalation branch, or memory is touched, so it cannot alter a clinical outcome. The one risk (intent must precede state writes) is the pre-write-flush ordering invariant tests/unit/test_audit_trail.py already guards, here extended.
changes.rollback_plan: git revert the three commits. Removing intent.py + the route block + reverting the test restores authorization_submitted as event #0; no migration and no data change (the details JSON addition is purely additive).

verdicts.notes: Record-only audit change; deterministic suite 652 passed, ruff/mypy/PHI-guard clean, manifest validates. Live clinical gate at iter-7 HEAD: golden-set accuracy threshold PASSED and zero-hallucination GC-018/019 PASSED -- the prior-auth adjudication path chg-7 touches is unaffected, as predicted (no agent decision surface changed). One unrelated clinical-suite failure, sme_authoring_smoke_test::test_sme_agent_smoke_round_trip, is PRE-EXISTING on origin/main: it hardcodes case_id 'GC-SMOKE', which the SME-authoring GC-NNN id validator (src/pacca/agents/sme_authoring/validators.py) rejects -- a subsystem chg-7 does not touch (diff = intent.py, authorizations.py, test_audit_trail.py, pyproject.toml, manifest, DECISIONS). Flagged for David separately; not a chg-7 regression.
